Section Title: What Fraud Issue is the Customer Experiencing
Content:
1. Confirm Account Secured upon Transfer
    1. Verify with Consumer Protection that the tokens were revoked on the account and has been properly secured. This process will force a logout on all active sessions, securing the account.
      1. If Consumer Protection did not revoke token access, the account is still compromised, or you feel it necessary - revoke token access.
        1. In the Freshdesk Ticket, fill out the following: [[ESCALATE:intro-esc]]
  2. Go to the corresponding dropdown section below for the specific unauthorized action taken.

Section Title: What Fraud Issue is the Customer Experiencing
Escalate Type: Customer Has No Service - Fraud / Account Takeover

Section Subtitle: Legitimate device replaced by a fraudulent one. — For eSIM devices
Content: Prior steps must be completed to secure the customer's account before proceeding.

Section Subtitle: Legitimate device replaced by a fraudulent one. — For eSIM devices
Content: These issues should be warm transferred to Wireless Case Management from Consumer Protection and a ticket should be created by Consumer Protection.

Section Subtitle: Legitimate device replaced by a fraudulent one. — For eSIM devices
Content:
1. Reactivate service to the legitimate device
    1. Verify the IMEI of legitimate device
    2. Verify the IMEI of the fraudulent device has been blacklisted
    3. If the device is eSIM compatible, reactivate in Customer App by sending a new eSIM profile
      1. Once installed, confirm services are working properly and move to the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section.

Section Subtitle: Legitimate device replaced by a fraudulent one. — For eSIM devices
Content: Device is pSIM instead? Use the {{▿Unauthorized Device/IMEI Swap - pSIM Devices Only->device-swap-psim}} section below.

Section Title: Unauthorized Device/IMEI Swap - pSIM Devices Only
Content:
1. Confirm Consumer Protection has completed the following:
    1. Account Verified: Identity and account ownership confirmed
    2. IMEIs Confirmed: Legitimate vs. Fraudulent devices identified
    3. Ticket Created: Ensure you receive the ticket number with the transfer
    4. Fraudulent Device Blacklisted: IMEI updated to BLACKLIST status in SCOUT
      1. If BLACKLIST was not done by Consumer Protection OR was done incorrectly, move to the {{▿Blacklist/Whitelist in SCOUT->scout}} section
  2. AT&T MVNO
    1. Gather the IMEI and ICCID of the legitimate device.
    2. Submit the appropriate Network Ticket.
      1. Description of Issue: Customer's line was taken over by a fraudster. Please reinstate the following equipment:
        1. IMEI: [Legitimate IMEI]
        2. ICCID: [Legitimate ICCID]
      2. Issue Type: Lost/Stolen
      3. Add the Case ID to the Freshdesk ticket.
      4. Set the Freshdesk status to Waiting on Third Party.
  3. T-Mobile
    1. Gather the IMEI and ICCID of the legitimate device.
    2. Navigate to the 'T-Mobile NextGen' Tool.
    3. Enter MSISDN of the impacted customer, and click search.
    4. Click the MSISDN to access the account.
    5. Verify the Previous SIM matches the legitimate customer's pSIM by obtaining the ICCID from the legitimate device itself.
      1. If the pSIM matches Previous SIM, continue.
      2. If the pSIM does not match Previous SIM, go to {{▿Getting a New pSIM->getting-new-psim}} section.
    6. Navigate to 'Change back to previous MSISDN & SIM' on the top right of the page.
    7. When the pop-up appears, verify the previous SIM is the legitimate ICCID.
    8. Click 'Change to Previous'.
    9. Once complete, power cycle the device and confirm services are working. Then follow the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section.
  4. Hybrid Mobile Network (HMN)
    1. Go to the {{▿Getting a New pSIM->getting-new-psim}} section.

1. Navigate to Customer App > Device Management > Order new pSIM and submit an order for the customer.
    1. If the customer chooses to purchase the pSIM at a Boost Retail store, be advised there is a $10 fee to purchase the pSIM and a $35 device setup fee if the store assists the customer with setting up the device.
  2. Advise the customer to call back in to care once they receive the new pSIM for activation assistance.
    1. For Customer App SIM card orders, offer to send eLiterature for how to activate their new SIM Card.
      1. Wireless Knowledge > App Library > Send eLiterature
  3. Instruct the customer to update information to secure their account once they are able to.
  4. Set the Freshdesk status to Resolved and follow the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section.

Section Subtitle: A plan change was made without the permission of the account holder.
Content: Prior steps must be completed to secure the customer's account before proceeding

Section Subtitle: A plan change was made without the permission of the account holder.
Content:
1. Identify the previous plan on the account prior to the unauthorized plan change
    1. In Wavelo Paddock, search for the line by MSISDN or Account UUID
    2. Once the subscription information is visible, click Transactions
    3. Here you will see the Transaction Date, Product ID and Product Description
    4. Obtain the Product ID of the plan prior to the unauthorized change
  2. Move to the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section

Section Subtitle: New lines added to the account without permission.
Content: Prior steps must be completed to secure the customer's account before proceeding

Section Subtitle: New lines added to the account without permission.
Content:
1. Identify the line that needs to be removed from the account
  2. Set the fraudulent line to disconnect in CApp
  3. Inform the customer that their line will be disconnected at the end of the cycle
  4. Blacklist the device associated with the unauthorized line added using the {{▿Blacklist/Whitelist in SCOUT->scout}} section.
  5. Move to the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section

Section Subtitle: The customer's phone number was ported out without their consent / knowledge
Content: Prior steps must be completed to secure the customer's account before proceeding

Section Subtitle: The customer's phone number was ported out without their consent / knowledge
Content:
1. Check for existing 'Unauthorized Port Out' ticket
    1. If the device is eSIM compatible
      1. Reactivate in Customer App by sending a new eSIM profile
    2. If none exists, create a 'Unauthorized Port Out' ticket
      1. Close the Fraud ticket / do not create another type of ticket
      2. Work the 'Unauthorized Port out Ticket' following the Unauthorized Port Ticketsopen_in_new article
    3. Move to the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section

Section Subtitle: This issue should not be handled by Wireless Case Management
Content: This issue should not be handled by Wireless Case Management

Section Subtitle: This issue should not be handled by Wireless Case Management
Content:
1. If a ticket does not already exist, do not create a ticket for this issue.
  2. Check Customer App to determine if the impacted PTN is in a 'Line Restricted' status.
  3. Check Customer App Memos to determine if Usage Fraud suspended the line.
    1. If the line is 'Line Restricted' by Usage Fraud, route the ticket to Usage Fraud.
      1. If the line is restricted due to ATO, include in the notes:
        1. "The customer's line was restricted due to account takeover. Please take this into consideration when reviewing this line so we restore service to the legitimate device."
    2. If the line is 'Line Restricted' by other means, continue.
    3. Advise the customer must email usageteam@boostmobile.com for assistance and apologize for any inconvenience.
      1. Inform the customer to include the following information in the email:
        1. Phone Number (PTN)
        2. Account Number
        3. Email Address associated with the account

Section Subtitle: Any other Fraud issue that is not one of the categories above
Content:
1. Determine if this is a Fraud related issue
    1. If not Fraud related, follow the appropriate steps to resolve the customer's issue
    2. If Fraud related, and it is none of the above categories
      1. Attempt to resolve the customer's issue
        1. If unable to, Move to the {{▿Fraud Routing->fraud-routing}} section and route to the appropriate Fraud team.
Important! This section should only be used when the fraud issue requires Fraud intervention or the customer has been made whole.

Subsection Title: If the Customer has Been Made Whole
Content:
Ex: Service has been restored, no credits or further intervention is required from WCM

  1. Disposition the Freshdesk ticket accordingly and notate
    1. Status: Resolved
    2. Support Status: Reconciliation
  2. Route the Freshdesk ticket to the appropriate Fraud team
  3. Add a private note to the Freshdesk ticket following the template below: [[TEMPLATE:made-whole]]

Template Text:
Group routing to:
Any Unauthorized Changes made by Fraudster:
PTN customer received fraudulent contact from:
Actions taken by WCM:
IMEI of the blacklisted device:
*The customer has been made whole. Fraud please conduct an investigation and route to Wireless Case Management if customer contact is needed.*

Subsection Title: If the Customer Requires Fraud Intervention to Fix an Issue
Content:
1. Disposition the Freshdesk ticket accordingly and notate
    1. Status: Open
  2. Route the Freshdesk ticket to the appropriate Fraud team
  3. Add a private note to the Freshdesk ticket following the template below: [[TEMPLATE:needs-intervention]]

Template Text:
Routing Ticket to another Group
Group routing to:
Any Account/Plan Changes made by Fraudster:
PTN customer received Fraudulent contact from:
Actions taken by WCM:
Action needed:
Group to reassign to upon updates:
Agent to assign to:

Section Subtitle: Use the information below to determine which Fraud team handles the issue the customer is experiencing
Content:
1. Customer claims device was "Hacked"
  2. Unauthorized Device/SIM Change
  3. Customer received an SMS or Email regarding unauthorized account activity/access
  4. Unable to access customer's account due to lock
  5. Unauthorized Changes to account
  6. Unauthorized Line Added (Added via Web, App or Agent)
  7. Unauthorized Add-On
  8. Unauthorized Base Plan Change
  9. Domestic Dispute
    1. In situations involving family members, partners, etc
      1. Inform the customer about splitting 1 or more phone numbers off of this account, and assist them if this is the request

Section Subtitle: Use the information below to determine which Fraud team handles the issue the customer is experiencing
Content:
1. In-Store Transactions
  2. Upset over plan change, misinformation, or device purchase at a store location
  3. Any Fraud Issue where a customer suspects a Retailer is at fault

Section Subtitle: Use the information below to determine which Fraud team handles the issue the customer is experiencing
Content:
1. Disputing an account suspension (Line Restriction)
  2. Unauthorized Port Out
  3. Porting Fraud

Blacklisting a device in SCOUT will remove the ability for a device to be activated on any network. This action should only be performed in account takeover situations.

  1. Blacklist the fraudulent IMEI in SCOUT
    1. PIMS Support > Disposition
    2. Enter the fraudulent IMEI
    3. On the right side, click 'Edit'
    4. Adjust the Current Disposition to: BLACKLIST
    5. Click 'Save'
    6. After blacklisting, the Disposition Status should say BLACKLIST:

Whitelisting a device in SCOUT should only be used to correct a status that was incorrectly dispositioned. Ex) A legitimate device was marked as BLACKLIST.

  1. Whitelist the fraudulent IMEI in SCOUT
    1. PIMS Support > Disposition
    2. Enter the fraudulent IMEI
    3. On the right side, click 'Edit'
    4. Adjust the Current Disposition to: WHITELIST
    5. Click 'Save'
    6. After whitelisting, the Disposition Status should say WHITELIST.
